mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance increases real borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite unemployment rising > 0.3pp in 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while simultaneously housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,400 thousand units, indicating weakening consumer demand and housing investment. This joint deterioration in demand signals reduced corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth < 2% AND HOUST < 1400 thousand units
regime_hint: demand-constrained environment with synchronized weakness in consumption and housing
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of instances when both RSAFS YoY < 2% and HOUST < 1400, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month for 2+ consecutive months, signaling supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization. This contraction in productive capacity constrains earnings growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as markets reprrice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines MoM for 2+ consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances when INDPRO declines MoM for 2+ consecutive months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100 thousand jobs per month while the unemployment rate remains below 4.0%, indicating a tightening labor market with slowing job creation. This combination signals wage pressure and potential Fed policy persistence, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as inflation expectations remain elevated.
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market with wage inflation pressures
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 62% of instances when PAYEMS MoM < 100k and UNRATE < 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates month-over-month by more than 0.4% while the federal funds rate remains below 5.5%, indicating real rates are insufficiently restrictive. This gap between inflation and policy rates signals continued monetary tightening ahead, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as markets anticipate further rate increases.
regime_hint: inflation-fighting monetary tightening cycle with real rates below neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 58% of instances when CPIAUCSL MoM > 0.4% and FEDFUNDS < 5.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate in the most recent quarter while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,300 thousand units, indicating synchronized weakness in aggregate demand and capital formation. This dual contraction produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 1.5% AND HOUST < 1300 thousand
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown with synchronized demand weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when GDPC1 annualized growth < 1.5% and HOUST < 1300, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) grows at an annualized rate exceeding 3% over a 3-month period while nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) expands by more than 150 thousand jobs per month, indicating robust supply-side expansion and productivity gains. This synchronized supply-side strength produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as earnings growth accelerates.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND PAYEMS MoM > 150k
regime_hint: post-shock recovery or early-cycle expansion with strong productivity
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 45% of instances when INDPRO 3-month annualized growth > 3% and PAYEMS MoM > 150k, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
